response.description:
? Did you know? Research by Dr. Art Kohn at Duke University shows that people forget up to 90% of what they learn within one week if they don’t revisit or apply it.

That means if you binge-watch this course and move on, you could lose almost all of your investment—in time and energy.

The difference between average learners and the top 1%—consultants, entrepreneurs, high-performers—is that they don’t just consume a course; they live it.

This program is designed as your 3-Level Transformation Map—taking you from Knowledge → Skill → Identity.

Through powerful questions, real-world lessons, habit systems, practical frameworks, mental models, and real-life challenges, you’ll learn to not only retain information but turn it into lasting action.

If you’re ready to make this course the launchpad for your next-level career and life, it starts here.

response.description: Stanford research shows leaders who embrace Shoshin (Beginner’s Mind) adapt to new tech 10x faster than those fixed in past expertise. This lecture dives into how CEOs like Satya Nadella used Growth Mindset + Spaced Repetition to reinvent Microsoft, proving that mental agility is the #1 predictor of success in an AI-disrupted world.

This lecture will help you assess your current leadership capabilities, identify areas for improvement, and set a roadmap for growth. Studies show that leaders who regularly evaluate their skills using structured feedback see a 19% increase in overall team performance, and 72% of high-performing leaders use self-assessment tools to continuously improve their leadership impact.

This practical and data-driven approach will empower you to take charge of your growth, leverage your strengths, and address any blind spots—ultimately elevating your leadership performance and transforming how you lead others.

"How to Set Yourself to Become Successful in the Probation Period" is designed to help you make the most of your first few months in a new role, ensuring that you not only meet but exceed expectations. The probation period is your chance to shine, with 78% of new hires stating that their success in this phase strongly influences their long-term career trajectory. Moreover, employees who actively seek feedback and demonstrate adaptability during probation have a 23% higher chance of securing a permanent role.

This session will equip you with the tools and mindset to excel from day one. You’ll learn how to clarify expectations, build positive relationships, showcase your skills, and quickly adapt to your new environment. We’ll also explore how to gather feedback and use it to accelerate your growth, allowing you to stand out as a high-performing team member.

By the end of this lecture, you’ll have a clear action plan to navigate your probation period with confidence, setting the stage for long-term success in your new role.

Are You Stuck in the TRAP of the EMPLOYEE MINDSET Without Even Realizing It?


Studies show only 14% of employees feel highly engaged at work — often because they unknowingly adopt limiting beliefs that block innovation, ownership, and career growth (Gallup). This eye-opening lecture reveals the 5 critical mindset traps holding you back and shows you how to shift from passive worker to proactive value creator, boosting your career acceleration by up to 3x (Harvard Business Review).


Are you ready to break free and think like a high-impact professional — not just an employee?

response.description:
"7 Core Strategies for Leading Yourself" is designed to help you build the foundation for strong, effective leadership by starting with the most important person—yourself. Leaders who consistently apply self-leadership techniques see a 29% increase in personal productivity, and individuals who practice self-leadership report being 32% more resilient in times of challenge and change.


In this session, we’ll explore seven powerful strategies that successful leaders use to manage their mindset, habits, and personal growth. You’ll learn how to set clear goals, maintain focus, hold yourself accountable, and continuously develop your skills. We’ll also cover emotional intelligence and how self-awareness can lead to better decision-making and stronger relationships, both personally and professionally.


By mastering these core strategies, you’ll gain the confidence and tools to not only lead yourself effectively but also inspire those around you. This lecture will give you the blueprint to align your actions with your ambitions, creating sustained success and growth in all areas of your life.

"Micro Productivity and Managing Your Time to Create More Value" focuses on using small, manageable productivity techniques to transform how you work and create impact. Research shows that breaking tasks into smaller segments, or micro-tasks, can increase personal productivity by 25%, while people who use effective time management strategies are 30% more likely to meet their professional goals.


In this session, you'll discover how to implement micro productivity techniques to maximize the value of your time, turning brief moments into opportunities for progress. We’ll cover methods for breaking down complex tasks into achievable steps, prioritizing high-impact activities, and eliminating distractions. You’ll also learn how to design your day around focused work periods, boosting both efficiency and output without burning out.


By the end of this lecture, you’ll have the tools to take control of your time and productivity, ensuring that every moment is used to its fullest potential. You’ll see how micro productivity can lead to massive results, driving both personal and professional success.

"Manage Your Energy to Produce Better Outcomes" explores the crucial link between energy management and performance enhancement. Research indicates that individuals who effectively manage their energy levels can experience a 40% increase in productivity, while those who focus on energy rather than just time report a 25% boost in overall job satisfaction.


In this session, you'll learn strategies to optimize your physical, mental, and emotional energy throughout the day. We’ll discuss techniques for recognizing your natural energy patterns, identifying activities that drain or replenish your energy, and creating routines that align with your peak performance times. Additionally, you'll discover how to integrate breaks and mindful practices to maintain high energy levels and prevent burnout.


By the end of this lecture, you’ll have actionable insights to enhance your energy management skills, leading to improved outcomes in both your personal and professional life. With a focused approach to energy, you’ll find yourself more engaged, productive, and ultimately more successful in achieving your goals.

response.description:
"Managing Your Mental Health to Become Consistent" delves into the critical connection between mental wellness and achieving consistency in your personal and professional life. Studies show that individuals who prioritize their mental health can see a 50% improvement in task consistency and are 30% more likely to achieve their goals over time compared to those who do not.


In this session, you'll explore practical strategies for managing mental health, including mindfulness techniques, stress management practices, and self-care routines that foster resilience. We’ll discuss how recognizing and addressing mental health challenges can help you maintain focus and motivation, enabling you to perform consistently even during stressful times. You'll learn how to set realistic expectations and cultivate a positive mindset that supports your long-term success.


By the end of this lecture, you will have the tools to proactively manage your mental health, leading to greater consistency in your efforts and outcomes. With a strong mental foundation, you’ll be better equipped to navigate challenges and maintain the momentum needed to achieve your goals, fostering a more balanced and productive life.
response.title: Mind Over Matter: Strategies for Consistency Through Mental Health Care

response.description:
"Master Your Note-Taking with a Simple Strategy and Free Tool" focuses on elevating your note-taking skills to boost learning and retention. Research shows that effective note-taking can improve information retention by up to 34% and enhance overall academic performance by 20%.


In this session, you’ll learn a straightforward yet powerful note-taking strategy that maximizes comprehension and minimizes time spent reviewing notes. We’ll introduce a free tool that streamlines your note-taking process, allowing for easy organization, quick access, and effective review. You’ll discover techniques for summarizing key points, using visuals for better recall, and integrating this tool into your daily workflow.


By the end of this lecture, you will be equipped with practical skills to enhance your note-taking efficiency, leading to improved learning outcomes and greater productivity. Whether you’re a student, professional, or lifelong learner, mastering this simple strategy will help you capture and retain knowledge more effectively, paving the way for your success.
response.title: Mastering Notes: A Simple Strategy and Free Tool for Enhanced Learning

response.description:
In the Industry 4.0 era, where AI, automation, and digital ecosystems redefine work, traditional leadership models are no longer enough. Studies show that over 80% of organizations investing in Leadership 4.0 — blending digital fluency, agility, and human-centered leadership — see faster innovation and stronger team performance (World Economic Forum). This lecture unpacks why Leadership 4.0 isn’t optional — it’s the key to staying relevant, resilient, and ready for the future of work.


Are you ready to evolve into the leader tomorrow demands?
response.title: Why Leadership 4.0 is a Game-Changer for Professionals Now?

is a fundamental thing for survival of a leader moving forward in this world.

response.description:
Are You Equipped with the 3 ESSENTIAL PRINCIPLES of DIGITAL LEADERSHIP Needed to Thrive in Today’s Transformation Era?


87% of companies say digital transformation is their top priority, yet only 30% succeed — often due to a lack of future-ready leadership (McKinsey). This powerful lecture breaks down 3 critical principles every leader must master to lead with agility, foster innovation, and drive digital-first strategies that deliver results. Learn how to build adaptive teams, embrace data-driven decision-making, and create a culture of continuous transformation.


Are you ready to become the digital leader your organization needs to succeed?
response.title: 3 Principle of Digital Leadership in this Era of Hyper Transformation

requests.response:
WEBVTT

00:00.050 --> 00:05.750
We want to be a leader 4.0 to adopting the leadership 4.0 methodologies.

00:05.780 --> 00:11.720
So to become a leader 4.0, I want to share with you three fundamental principles right now.

00:11.720 --> 00:13.760
And it's not going to be just a principle.

00:13.760 --> 00:17.480
I also want you to do a roleplay activity with me as well.

00:17.510 --> 00:21.290

And we're going to we're going to work together with this one to get you moving forward on that one.

So at this point you can pause this session right now and download the resource section right now on

00:31.190 --> 00:35.780
the resource section, and start reading and discussing with me on this.

00:36.110 --> 00:43.640
The first principle, replacing the traditional decision making to use AI powered decision making.

00:43.940 --> 00:49.460
So let me just break it down for a traditional leader who typically make decisions based on their years

00:49.460 --> 00:50.540
of experience.

00:50.690 --> 00:56.870
The other side, the new leadership is about using the AI to get thousands or millions of data points

00:56.870 --> 01:03.800
and get a lot of operational decisions, and finally, to choose the best decision for themselves.

01:03.830 --> 01:06.590
And this is what JP Morgan is doing really well.

01:06.620 --> 01:14.680
They have reviewing all their contracts by eye, which is getting it done for seconds, which used to

01:14.680 --> 01:19.720
take them 360,000 hours to get it done per year.

01:19.720 --> 01:22.390
And that's why you could do this.

01:22.420 --> 01:30.040
Use AI powered tools like ChatGPT, Tableau or Watson to get a lot of AI powered decisions to support

01:30.040 --> 01:30.970
your decisions.

01:30.970 --> 01:32.950
And this is why you should not do.

01:32.980 --> 01:39.700
Let's stop making just intuition based decisions, because a leader who only makes the decisions based

01:39.700 --> 01:43.510
on the intuitions, they often make major mistakes.

01:44.050 --> 01:47.020
So now it's time for you to do this exercise.

01:47.050 --> 01:52.810
Now I want you to pause the video, go to the template and ready yourself, fill it up and then get

01:52.810 --> 01:55.000
back to me about this principle.

02:05.710 --> 02:12.190
The second principle is about the data is the most valuable asset for the new leaders.

02:12.190 --> 02:14.470
So now what is the old leader used to do?

02:14.500 --> 02:20.620
Old leader used to make decisions based on their opinion, experience and gut feeling.

02:20.740 --> 02:26.950
The new leadership is about using data driven approach to make every possible decision moving forward.

02:26.980 --> 02:28.780
And here is a great success story.

02:28.810 --> 02:38.950
Netflix saved 1 billion USD in one year by using AI to to predict what people want to use, compared

response.description:
Lack of vision leads to demotivation, confusion, and underperformance. This session provides a step-by-step exercise to craft a powerful vision that gives your team purpose, direction, and focus.

Learn how to connect your team’s goals to a shared vision, eliminate uncertainty, and boost engagement by giving every member a clear sense of purpose. This proven framework will help you solve motivation gaps and ensure clarity across your organization.

By the end, you’ll walk away with a practical vision statement that acts as a north star, empowering your team to stay driven and focused on achieving long-term success.
response.title: Vision Crafting Blueprint: Solve Motivation and Clarity Issues in Team as leader

response.description:
This session reveals the power of feedback loops in driving continuous improvement and high performance within your team. Research shows that structured feedback can increase productivity by up to 39%, yet most leaders fail to use it effectively.

In this lecture, you’ll discover a proven framework for implementing feedback loops that spark growth and innovation. Learn how to give actionable feedback, track progress, and adjust strategies in real time to achieve measurable results.

By the end, you’ll have the tools to create a culture of continuous improvement, empowering your team to reach new performance levels with clarity and confidence.
response.title: The Feedback Loop Formula: Unlock Team Performance with Science-Based Leadership

response.description:
Without strong decision-making skills, teams often face paralysis, miscommunication, and inefficiency. This session introduces a science-based approach to train your team in making faster, smarter, and more aligned decisions.

You’ll learn how to recognize decision-making bottlenecks, implement frameworks for risk assessment, and ensure clarity when handling complex challenges. Discover how to create a culture where every team member feels empowered to make critical decisions with confidence.

Walk away with actionable strategies to enhance team performance, reduce delays, and increase accountability through better decision-making.
response.title: Step by Step Guideline to Develop Decision Making ability in the team

response.description:
Teams with a growth mindset are 43% more likely to show higher engagement, creativity, and problem-solving abilities. But building this mindset requires intentional effort from leaders.

In this session, you’ll learn step-by-step strategies to shift your team’s mindset from fixed to growth-focused. Explore science-backed techniques to reshape team behaviors, handle failure constructively, and foster a culture of continuous learning.

Discover how to apply simple tools like feedback loops, self-assessment exercises, and brainstorming frameworks to drive positive change and boost performance across your team.

Leave with practical guidelines to help your team stay adaptable, innovative, and resilient in the face of challenges.
response.title: Strategy, tools and guide to boost growth mindset inside the team as leader

response.description:
"Tactics for Boosting Your Productivity" delves into actionable strategies that can significantly enhance your efficiency and output. Research indicates that implementing effective productivity tactics can lead to a 25% increase in work output and can help individuals reduce their workweek by up to 15 hours without sacrificing quality.


In this session, you'll explore a variety of proven tactics designed to streamline your workflow, eliminate distractions, and prioritize tasks effectively. We’ll cover techniques such as the Pomodoro Technique, time blocking, and the Eisenhower Matrix to help you focus on what truly matters. You'll also learn how to leverage technology and tools to automate repetitive tasks, enabling you to devote more time to high-impact activities.


By the end of this lecture, you’ll be equipped with a toolkit of practical strategies to boost your productivity and create a more organized and efficient work environment. Whether you’re looking to enhance personal effectiveness or lead a high-performing team, these tactics will empower you to achieve your goals faster and with greater ease.
response.title: Elevate Your Game: Tactical Approaches to Skyrocket Productivity

response.description:
Your personal brand shapes your professional journey. It’s the lens through which colleagues, leaders, and decision-makers perceive your value. In this dynamic session, we’ll uncover the key strategies to build and protect your brand while avoiding common traps that derail careers.


The Risks of Poor Branding

An unclear or negative personal brand can stunt career growth. According to a LinkedIn study, professionals with inconsistent personal brands—such as a lack of visibility or unclear expertise—are 25% less likely to be promoted. Research by Payscale reveals that individuals perceived as unreliable or lacking leadership presence earn salaries 10–15% lower than peers who project strong, cohesive brands​


The Power of a Strong Brand

On the flip side, a compelling personal brand can fast-track success. A survey by CareerBuilder found that 70% of employers value strong online personal brands when considering promotions. Professionals who actively build their reputation through thought leadership and consistent work presence are twice as likely to secure leadership roles and often negotiate 10–20% higher salaries than their counterparts​


Key Takeaways:

  • Recognize common pitfalls that damage personal branding and learn how to avoid them.

  • Discover actionable steps to build a visible, consistent, and inspiring personal brand.

  • Use your brand to unlock promotions, leadership roles, and higher earnings.

By mastering your personal brand, you’ll position yourself as a trusted and influential leader—ready to seize every opportunity and thrive in Leadership 4.0.
response.title: Protect Your Reputation: Avoiding Common Traps in Personal Branding

response.description:
"Avoid These Traps and Mistakes to Save Your Personal Brand" is a critical exploration of the common pitfalls that can undermine your personal brand and reputation. Studies reveal that 70% of employers Google candidates before making hiring decisions, and a significant 60% of individuals have faced reputational damage due to preventable mistakes in their online presence.


In this session, you'll learn about the key traps to avoid, such as inconsistent messaging, neglecting online engagement, and failing to monitor your digital footprint. We’ll provide actionable insights on how to cultivate a strong, authentic personal brand that resonates with your target audience. You'll discover strategies for maintaining professionalism across platforms, effectively managing your online image, and building a network of supportive connections.


By the end of this lecture, you'll be equipped with the knowledge and tools to protect and enhance your personal brand. Whether you’re an emerging professional or an established leader, understanding these pitfalls will empower you to navigate the complexities of personal branding successfully, ensuring that your reputation aligns with your goals and values.

response.description:
"7 Hacks to Get Started as a New Manager" is designed to equip new leaders with the essential tools and insights needed to thrive in their roles from day one. Studies show that 60% of new managers feel unprepared for their leadership responsibilities, and effective onboarding can improve retention rates by 20%.


In this session, you will uncover seven actionable hacks that will help you transition smoothly into your management role. From establishing clear communication channels and setting expectations with your team to leveraging feedback mechanisms and cultivating a supportive environment, these strategies are tailored to enhance your leadership effectiveness.


You’ll also learn how to build rapport with your team, navigate challenges, and create a culture of accountability and collaboration. By the end of this lecture, you will be equipped with practical techniques to empower yourself and your team, ensuring a successful start to your management journey. Whether you're stepping into your first managerial position or looking to refine your leadership approach, these hacks will set you on the path to becoming an effective and respected manager.
response.title: Lead Like a Pro: 7 Powerful Hacks for New Managers

response.description:
"7 Principles to Shine Your Leadership Ability" focuses on fundamental concepts that can transform you into an exceptional leader. Research indicates that 90% of high-performing leaders practice effective communication, and organizations with strong leadership see a 30% increase in employee engagement and performance.


In this session, you will explore seven essential principles that can help you enhance your leadership capabilities, including the importance of emotional intelligence, authenticity, and a growth mindset. Each principle will provide practical insights on how to cultivate a positive team environment, inspire others, and navigate challenges with resilience and confidence.


By the end of this lecture, you will possess a robust framework for developing your leadership skills and fostering a culture of excellence within your team. Whether you're an aspiring leader or a seasoned professional looking to refine your approach, these principles will empower you to shine in your leadership role, driving meaningful results and inspiring those around you.

response.description:
"Strategies to Manage Priorities for Your Team to Produce Great Outcomes" delves into the critical skill of prioritization, a key factor in driving team success. Research shows that teams that effectively prioritize tasks can improve productivity by up to 25%, while organizations with clear priority management report a 30% increase in project completion rates.


In this session, you will learn actionable strategies for establishing and managing priorities within your team, including techniques such as the Eisenhower Matrix, setting SMART goals, and implementing regular check-ins. You’ll discover how to align team efforts with organizational objectives, delegate effectively, and create a culture of accountability.


By the end of this lecture, you'll be equipped with the tools to help your team focus on what truly matters, ensuring that their efforts lead to exceptional outcomes. Whether you're a new manager or an experienced leader, mastering these strategies will empower you to drive performance and achieve your team's goals with clarity and confidence.
response.title: Strategic Prioritization: Unlocking Your Team's Potential for Outstanding Result

response.description:
"Skills for Building High Productivity in Your Team" focuses on the essential capabilities that drive team performance and enhance productivity. Studies show that organizations with highly productive teams can experience 40% more revenue growth and report a 60% reduction in project timelines due to efficient collaboration and effective skill utilization.


In this session, you will explore key skills that leaders must cultivate within their teams, including effective communication, time management, and goal-setting techniques. You’ll learn how to foster a culture of continuous improvement and collaboration, leverage individual strengths, and implement strategies for accountability and motivation.


By the end of this lecture, you will have a toolkit of practical skills to help your team achieve their highest potential and drive outstanding results. Whether you're leading a small group or a large department, mastering these skills will empower you to create an environment where productivity thrives, enabling your team to deliver exceptional outcomes consistently.
response.title: Productivity Unleashed: Skills to Elevate Your Team's Performance

response.description:
"DFC Framework: Delegation, Feedback, and Coaching Up Your Team Member" explores a powerful approach to enhancing team performance through effective delegation, constructive feedback, and coaching. Research indicates that teams with strong delegation practices can boost productivity by 33%, while effective feedback can enhance employee engagement by 14%, leading to a more motivated workforce.


In this session, you will discover how to implement the DFC framework to empower your team members, including best practices for delegating tasks, providing timely and constructive feedback, and coaching for growth. You’ll learn how to identify individual strengths, align tasks with team members’ skill sets, and create a feedback-rich environment that promotes continuous learning and improvement.


By the end of this lecture, you will have actionable strategies to elevate your leadership effectiveness and cultivate a high-performing team. Whether you are a new manager or an experienced leader, mastering the DFC framework will enable you to unlock the full potential of your team, resulting in enhanced performance, increased accountability, and sustained success.
response.title: Mastering the DFC Framework: Delegation, Feedback, and Coaching for Team Success

response.description:
"How to Boost Motivation for Your Team" focuses on actionable strategies to elevate team morale and engagement, essential components for achieving high performance. Research shows that motivated teams can see productivity increase by 25%, and organizations with high employee engagement report a 21% higher profitability.


In this session, you will learn effective techniques for boosting motivation, including setting clear goals, recognizing achievements, and fostering a positive work environment. You’ll discover how to tailor your approach to meet individual team members' needs and leverage intrinsic motivators, such as purpose and autonomy, to create a culture of enthusiasm and commitment.


By the end of this lecture, you will be equipped with practical tools to inspire and energize your team, enabling them to perform at their best. Whether you're a new manager or an experienced leader, implementing these strategies will help you cultivate an environment where motivation thrives, leading to improved performance and greater organizational success.
response.title: Fueling Success: Proven Techniques to Enhance Team Motivation

response.description:
"Managing the Productivity of a Hybrid Team While Team Members Work from Home" delves into effective strategies for enhancing productivity in a hybrid work environment. Studies show that organizations with hybrid work models can experience a 20% increase in productivity and report 87% of employees feeling more productive when working remotely due to reduced commuting and improved work-life balance.


In this session, you will explore best practices for managing a hybrid team, including setting clear expectations, utilizing technology for seamless communication, and fostering a strong team culture that transcends physical boundaries. You'll learn how to implement productivity metrics, ensure accountability, and encourage collaboration among team members, regardless of their location.


By the end of this lecture, you will have practical tools and insights to effectively lead your hybrid team, ensuring that every member remains engaged, motivated, and productive. Whether you're a seasoned manager or new to hybrid leadership, these strategies will empower you to navigate the challenges of remote work while maximizing your team's potential.
response.title: "Thriving in Hybrid: Strategies for Managing Productivity from Anywhere

response.description:
82% of first-time managers say they find it hard to give up control—and it’s holding them back by wasting time, eroding trust, and stalling their team’s development.

In this session, we’ll explore the key mental barriers that make delegation difficult—like ego, perfectionism, and fear of losing relevance. You’ll discover why always playing the “hero” role does more harm than good, and how to shift your mindset using the 80/20 Leadership Lens for smarter, more empowering leadership.
response.title: Evolving Your Mindset: Transitioning from Doing to Leading Through Delegation

requests.response:
WEBVTT

00:00.400 --> 00:03.600
Let me ask you one question very bluntly.

00:03.640 --> 00:11.600
What is the one big mistake almost every leader do every day would cost them a lot of money and end

00:11.600 --> 00:12.200
of the month.

00:12.480 --> 00:13.560
Do you know what is it?

00:13.600 --> 00:14.320
Can you guess?

00:14.600 --> 00:19.680
It's actually doing the thing they should have already delegated to team member.

00:19.880 --> 00:25.360
And actually, according to the Harvard Business Review, the research came out 78% leader confessed

00:25.360 --> 00:29.560
that one they are doing the thing which they should have delegated already.

00:29.840 --> 00:32.160
And here is the worst part about this thing.

00:32.520 --> 00:33.520
And here's the thing.

00:33.960 --> 00:44.000
Leaders who are delegating less almost 33% least productive compared to the other leaders who are delegating

00:44.040 --> 00:45.840
to the team effectively.

00:46.040 --> 00:51.400
So it's not just a time issue, it's also a major mindset issue as well.

00:51.800 --> 00:56.440
Once I was coaching one of the operation manager, very talented.

00:56.480 --> 00:57.080
Sharp.

00:57.680 --> 00:58.600
Her name is Lin.

00:58.600 --> 00:59.440
And what's the thing?

00:59.440 --> 01:00.720
What's happening with Lin is about?

01:00.720 --> 01:03.380
I was asking her why you're not delegating enough?

01:03.380 --> 01:07.420
And the answer is this I don't trust my team member.

01:07.420 --> 01:10.420
If I deliver to them, they will be delivering the right quality.

01:10.700 --> 01:11.500
That's the point.

01:11.740 --> 01:18.180
So moving forward, what I did next 3 to 4 weeks of time, I coached her and where and after those weeks

01:18.180 --> 01:25.420
of coaching and this is what had happened, lean was having almost ten hours per week free up doing

01:25.420 --> 01:26.380
all this work.

01:26.740 --> 01:32.900
And actually her team delivered two completed process without her for the operations.

01:32.900 --> 01:34.020
So what was the change?

01:34.140 --> 01:35.260
The change was this one.

01:35.260 --> 01:42.580
She stopped becoming the doer and become a much effective delegator, and that was the trigger point

01:42.580 --> 01:43.100
for her.

01:43.460 --> 01:45.300
So now let's break down.

01:45.300 --> 01:50.980
What are the key barrier is stopping as a leader to delegate more effectively.

01:51.180 --> 01:56.380
You probably have already seen a lot of them around you, but let me put it all together so you know

01:56.380 --> 01:57.580
the pattern clearly.

01:57.940 --> 02:04.620
So typically there are three mental barriers that actually holding managers to not to delegate effectively.

02:04.910 --> 02:07.550
the first barrier is actually ego.

02:07.710 --> 02:10.310
You think no one can do this like me?

02:10.590 --> 02:13.590
It feels good to be the go to person.

02:13.950 --> 02:18.590
But that mindset trap you in operational quicksand.

02:18.830 --> 02:20.710
You become the bottleneck.

02:20.990 --> 02:24.310
The second barrier is about fear of losing control.

02:24.670 --> 02:26.430
What if they mess up?

02:26.750 --> 02:29.870
What if it reflects badly on me?

02:30.310 --> 02:36.030
Delegation feels like risk, but in reality, not delegating is the bigger risk.

02:36.070 --> 02:39.990
Burnout, missed deadline and underdeveloped teams.

02:40.190 --> 02:43.390
The third barrier is I call perfectionism.

02:43.910 --> 02:48.430
You do rather just do it yourself because you think it will be faster.

02:48.430 --> 02:52.590
But long term, that makes you the single point of failure in your system.

02:52.830 --> 02:57.750
I have studied hundreds of great leaders, and there's one key thing I want to share with you is about

02:57.750 --> 02:58.670
the Great leader.

02:58.710 --> 03:05.550
Always apply 8020 rules, which is also known as Pareto principles for the leadership delegation aspect

03:05.550 --> 03:06.070
as well.

03:06.330 --> 03:11.970
spent 80% of your time on the 20% of the activities that drive the biggest outcome.

03:12.010 --> 03:13.890
Vision, strategy and coaching.

03:14.250 --> 03:20.010
Everything else should be delegated, delayed, automated, or even eliminated.

03:20.250 --> 03:24.610
Now what I want is to pause and ask yourself this.

03:24.890 --> 03:33.250
What's one task on your plate right now that you are holding onto, even though someone else could do

03:33.250 --> 03:35.010
it 80% as well?

03:35.930 --> 03:37.930
Why haven't you let it go?

03:38.330 --> 03:42.090
I'm going to give you total three pro tips which will help your delegation.

03:42.090 --> 03:43.050
Much more thing.

03:43.090 --> 03:44.090
It's not about delegation.

03:44.090 --> 03:45.130
It's not a matter of risk.

03:45.130 --> 03:48.130
It's about how you take the right bet in the right time.

03:48.490 --> 03:50.050
Give the right task in the right time.

03:50.090 --> 03:51.370
Sounds very complicated, right?

03:51.410 --> 03:53.290
But let me give you these three pro tip.

03:53.330 --> 03:56.610
And we're going to make it much easier for you in terms of delegation.

03:56.650 --> 03:57.610
Let's get started.

03:57.770 --> 04:00.010
The first one is delegate to develop.

04:00.130 --> 04:03.530
Stop thinking of delegation as just task sharing.

04:03.730 --> 04:06.130
Start seeing it as talent building.

04:06.330 --> 04:09.350
Every time you delegate, someone of your team grows.

04:09.390 --> 04:12.510
Number two, start with a low risk task.

04:12.750 --> 04:14.870
If you are nervous, start small.

04:15.390 --> 04:20.510
Delegate something that isn't high stakes but requires ownership.

04:20.710 --> 04:23.470
Number three don't delegate and just disappear.

04:23.510 --> 04:25.710
That's a kind of called abdication.

04:25.710 --> 04:29.230
That means you're just telling them to do how they're doing it.

04:29.270 --> 04:31.230
When they're doing it, you don't care about it.

04:31.230 --> 04:32.790
So I'm just thinking about it.

04:32.790 --> 04:34.230
An amazing outcome.

04:34.270 --> 04:35.870
So that's called an abdication.

04:35.870 --> 04:36.990
It's not really delegation.

04:36.990 --> 04:38.750
So that's the point I want to clear for you.

04:38.790 --> 04:41.630
A lot of managers right now in the trap of abdication right now.

04:41.790 --> 04:43.070
So don't get into that thing.

04:43.110 --> 04:46.670
Stay connected with checkpoints, not micromanagement.

04:47.030 --> 04:50.270
These build trust without sacrificing the performance.

04:50.430 --> 04:54.950
Now I want to pause for yourself and think about this question.

04:55.350 --> 05:02.950
If you had 30% more time this week, where would you invest it strategically now?

05:03.310 --> 05:06.670
What you need to let go to make that happen?

05:06.870 --> 05:09.150
I want you to answer this question now.

05:09.590 --> 05:12.690
Write down yourself in your computer, on a notebook, or whatever you have.

05:12.730 --> 05:16.970
Pause the video, write it down, and get back to me because this is going to be healthy for you to

05:17.010 --> 05:17.490
take it.

05:17.530 --> 05:19.330
Amazingly effective for you.

05:30.330 --> 05:33.850
Now I want to take a small action moment with you.

05:34.090 --> 05:35.610
I want you to do this.

05:36.010 --> 05:40.010
I want you to choose one task, even a very small micro task.

05:40.290 --> 05:42.290
You choose to delegate it.

05:42.690 --> 05:46.130
And then I want you to write down what is the thing?

05:46.170 --> 05:47.490
Is holding you back?

05:47.690 --> 05:50.930
What beliefs thing was holding you back to delegate that before?

05:51.170 --> 05:53.210
Now I want you to pause the video right now.

05:53.250 --> 06:00.330
Write it down your post and post it on LinkedIn profile and tag me into the post or in the comment section

06:00.330 --> 06:01.250
and I will get back to you.

06:01.290 --> 06:05.450
So now I'm going to pause the video right now, get it done, and get back to me in a few minutes.

06:05.450 --> 06:07.810
You can use ChatGPT to help to write your posts.

06:07.850 --> 06:08.370
Even better.

06:08.410 --> 06:09.250
No problem.

06:19.900 --> 06:21.460
Well done, you just made it happen.

06:21.460 --> 06:25.060
Your post and I'm looking forward to see your post and I will as soon I get the notification in LinkedIn.

06:25.060 --> 06:27.340
I'll get back to there and meet you there too.

06:27.740 --> 06:30.340
But let me just put you on this thing, right?

06:30.380 --> 06:36.260
Delegation is not about just only time saving, because every time you release any task to your team

06:36.260 --> 06:42.060
member, it's give you the opportunity to develop your leadership vision and do more high value activity

06:42.100 --> 06:42.420
too.

06:42.500 --> 06:47.540
And also on the other side, let your team to develop their capability and grow as well.

06:47.820 --> 06:50.220
You are not here as a leader to do everything.

06:50.220 --> 06:53.020
You are here to lead the people who can.

06:53.380 --> 06:55.740
And I'll see you in the next coaching session.

response.description:
90% of managers who fail to delegate strategically report spending over 60% of their time on low-value tasks.


In this session, you'll learn how to separate $10 tasks from $10K decisions with the Task Value Ladder and Eisenhower Matrix. We’ll also show you how to use AI as your first delegate—even before hiring a team.
response.title: Unlock Time for What Truly Matters

01:29.780 --> 01:36.500
So I'm going to give you three delegation framework and that will solve your delegation related problem.

01:36.500 --> 01:42.660
It will give you a clear timeline and clear path to delegate properly, so you can create a breakthrough

01:42.660 --> 01:44.940
result from your team through delegation.

01:44.940 --> 01:48.540
And because you free up to do more important thing ready?

Of course you do.

01:49.300 --> 01:50.820
Let's get started with the first one.

01:51.140 --> 01:53.940
The first one is about the delegation matrix.

01:54.100 --> 01:57.100

Urgency is now versus later, and importance is about high and low.

02:07.950 --> 02:10.470
Does this help someone on your team grow?

response.description:
Poor delegation leads to rework, delays, and frustration—and it’s not your team’s fault.


57% of employees say unclear instructions are their #1 complaint about delegation. This lecture introduces the SCCARD Framework, helping you structure every handoff with clarity and trust. From defining Success Metrics to setting Checkpoints, you’ll delegate once—and delegate well.
response.title: A Proven 6-Step Framework for Effective Delegation—Every Time

Micromanaging kills trust. But disappearing after delegating kills results.


Only 1 in 4 leaders follow up effectively after assigning a task. This session equips you with Trust Loops, micro check-in systems, and visual tools (like Kanban boards) to ensure accountability—without constant chasing.
response.title: Effective Follow-Up: Lead with Clarity, Not Micromanagement

requests.response:
WEBVTT

00:00.400 --> 00:01.320
Fully up.

00:01.360 --> 00:07.960
Should not feel like babysitting yet over 60% of the managers say their biggest frustration with the

00:07.960 --> 00:12.920
delegation is having to consistently check in or chase people.

00:13.320 --> 00:17.120
And good news here is the one according to the Google Manager project.

00:17.480 --> 00:24.560
The best manager would follow one pattern the don't micromanage to create a trust loop.

00:24.880 --> 00:28.880
Now, in this coaching session, I'm going to show you how you can do that as well.

00:29.240 --> 00:34.920
Once I was coaching one of the marketing manager, Jennifer, and one of the problem Jennifer came on

00:34.920 --> 00:37.080
in my coaching is to talk about this.

00:37.080 --> 00:43.320
Every time I delegate, I spend more time following up than if I just do it myself.

00:43.480 --> 00:45.760
The problem is not about the task.

00:45.760 --> 00:50.040
It's not having the right system to get them done with the right quality.

00:50.360 --> 00:56.560
So then I coached Jennifer, building on the right coaching reminder rhythm.

00:56.760 --> 01:02.960
So which solve the problem for Jennifer then moving forward in a few weeks period of time when every

01:03.000 --> 01:09.330
time Jennifer was delegating to her team member, Remember she was getting much more better outcome

01:09.330 --> 01:10.130
moving forward.

01:10.130 --> 01:16.050
And over time she came to a really good position where the team was always know how to get things done

01:16.090 --> 01:17.530
to the to her expectation.

01:17.650 --> 01:18.730
So what is the thing outcome?

01:18.770 --> 01:19.290
The outcome?

01:19.330 --> 01:24.490
How stress significantly dropped and outcome was a much more higher what she was looking for.

01:24.850 --> 01:26.530
So the question is about how can you get it done?

01:26.530 --> 01:33.570
I'm going to give you three frameworks which you can apply to create a great checking frameworks and

01:33.570 --> 01:34.450
follow up frameworks.

01:34.450 --> 01:39.850
And all the delegation will be much effective, so you don't have to chase through the goals moving

01:39.850 --> 01:40.290
forward.

01:40.570 --> 01:44.450
The first one is about weekly checking versus micro checking.

01:44.490 --> 01:49.330
Most managers default to two extremes.

01:49.370 --> 01:54.770
No follow up at all or constant checking is called a micro management.

01:54.890 --> 01:59.210
So what I want to tell you is about instead use a hybrid approach.

01:59.410 --> 02:07.730
So first weekly check in 20 to 30 minutes to review progress blockers, next steps, ideas for project

02:07.730 --> 02:10.050
alignment and the deeper support.

02:10.410 --> 02:17.170
The second one is micro check ins, quick slack or WhatsApp message, or a two minute hurdles to stay

02:17.210 --> 02:19.890
on radar without hovering.

02:20.250 --> 02:25.410
So here's the rule of thumb check in without checking up.

02:25.450 --> 02:27.970
The second framework I want to give you the trust loop.

02:28.010 --> 02:31.610
The Google Project manager significantly using that one really well.

02:31.850 --> 02:39.530
Google's Project Oxygen found that high performing teams thrive when there is a frequent, consistent,

02:39.570 --> 02:42.730
and non-controlled interaction.

02:42.970 --> 02:45.210
So we call that one a trust loop.

02:45.530 --> 02:48.130
And this is how the loop works.

02:48.290 --> 02:51.690
Step number one clear task with context.

02:51.930 --> 02:53.570
Number two agreed.

02:53.570 --> 02:54.490
Follow up rhythm.

02:54.490 --> 02:58.930
Number three feedback exchange both ways by the way.

02:59.530 --> 03:02.850
And number four is about recognition of progress.

03:03.010 --> 03:05.450
And number five is repeat.

03:05.650 --> 03:11.730
So this loop create psychological safety and ownership inside your team.

03:11.970 --> 03:16.570
And your team always feel they have been seen and validated and recognized.

03:16.850 --> 03:24.100
The third framework I'm gonna give you two is about KPI versus feedback and dashboard and signals.

03:24.100 --> 03:30.380
Managing follow up isn't just only about the data, it's about interpreting the right signals.

03:30.580 --> 03:35.260
So first KPIs quantitative result, example.

03:35.540 --> 03:37.220
Number of proposals sent.

03:37.260 --> 03:38.700
Lead close ticket.

03:38.700 --> 03:40.220
Resolve number.

03:40.260 --> 03:44.060
Type two is our soft signals tone in meetings.

03:45.020 --> 03:46.180
Small deadlines.

03:46.460 --> 03:48.420
Energy in updates.

03:48.420 --> 03:50.860
Use the dashboard for hard data.

03:51.340 --> 03:57.100
Use your ears, eyes, and sympathy and empathy for everything else.

response.description:
Welcome to Meeting 4.0, where you’ll gain the essential skills to lead productive, high-impact meetings that drive results and elevate your influence. In this introductory lecture, we’ll lay out the foundation for what’s to come. You’ll discover how mastering effective meetings can significantly accelerate your career growth, increase your influence across teams, and help you get things done with ease—even in cross-functional, complex organizations.

What You’ll Learn in This module:


  1. Why Meetings Matter: We’ll explore the underestimated power of meetings and how a well-run meeting can boost team productivity, foster collaboration, and support your growth as an emerging leader.

  2. My Personal Story: I’ll share how mastering meeting dynamics helped me fast-track my career—from manager at 23 to director at 29, leading teams of 160+. You’ll learn how I used meetings to create business breakthroughs and build influence that led to rapid promotions.

  3. Your Path to Leading with Impact: I’ll introduce the Meeting 4.0 methodology, our unique framework designed to help you lead clear, purposeful, and engaging meetings that inspire action and help you achieve your objectives.

  4. Skills You’ll Gain: By the end of this lecture, you’ll have a clear roadmap of the skills and strategies you’ll be equipped with by the end of this course, from leading without authority to handling challenging discussions with confidence.

This introductory lecture sets the stage for an empowering journey through Meeting 4.0. You’ll come away energized, with a clear understanding of how each module in this course will build your meeting leadership toolkit—and help you unlock new opportunities in your career.


Get ready to transform your approach to meetings and turn them into a tool for success.
response.title: What will you achieve from module 6?

response.description:
"Strategies to Use Meetings for Getting Things Done" focuses on how to transform meetings from mere discussions into actionable outcomes. Research indicates that effective meetings can increase team productivity by up to 30%, while poorly organized meetings can waste up to 50% of employees' time, leading to frustration and disengagement.


In this session, you will learn practical strategies for planning and executing productive meetings, including setting clear agendas, defining objectives, and assigning action items. You’ll discover techniques for engaging participants, fostering open communication, and ensuring accountability for follow-through.


By the end of this lecture, you will be equipped with the skills to conduct meetings that drive results, enhance collaboration, and align your team's efforts towards achieving organizational goals. Whether you are leading a small team or a large department, mastering these strategies will empower you to make meetings a valuable tool for productivity and success.
response.title: Maximize Impact: Transforming Meetings into Productivity Powerhouses

response.description:
"Blueprint of Your Highly Effective Meeting" outlines the essential components for creating meetings that yield tangible results. Studies show that effective meetings can improve team performance by up to 30%, while poorly organized meetings contribute to an average productivity loss of approximately 20% across the organization.


In this session, you will explore the critical elements of a successful meeting, including setting clear objectives, designing a focused agenda, and determining the right participants. You’ll learn how to utilize time management techniques to keep discussions on track, foster engagement among attendees, and ensure actionable outcomes through well-defined follow-ups.


By the end of this lecture, you will have a practical blueprint for conducting meetings that not only respect participants' time but also enhance collaboration and drive strategic initiatives. Whether you are new to leadership or looking to refine your meeting strategies, this session will equip you with the tools necessary to elevate your meetings and maximize their impact on your team's productivity and overall success.
response.title: The Ultimate Blueprint: Crafting Highly Effective Meetings

response.description:
"How to Start a Meeting as a Manager Which Produces High-Quality Outcomes" emphasizes the crucial role of effective meeting beginnings in achieving productive results. Research indicates that 70% of meeting outcomes are determined within the first five minutes, while well-structured openings can lead to a 25% increase in meeting productivity.


In this session, you will learn essential techniques for starting meetings on the right foot, including setting a clear agenda, establishing ground rules, and defining objectives. You will discover how to create an inclusive environment that encourages participation and engagement from all attendees right from the start.


By the end of this lecture, you will be equipped with practical tools and strategies to ensure your meetings begin with purpose, direction, and clarity. These skills will not only enhance the quality of your meetings but also empower your team to collaborate more effectively and achieve high-quality outcomes consistently. Whether you are an experienced manager or new to leadership, this session will help you unlock the potential of every meeting you lead.
response.title: The Art of Initiation: How to Launch Meetings that Drive Results

response.description:
"Approaches to Make Your Online Meeting Highly Effective as a Manager" focuses on essential strategies that can transform your virtual meetings into powerful platforms for collaboration and decision-making. Research shows that effective online meetings can boost team productivity by 23%, while poorly executed virtual meetings can lead to up to 50% of participants disengaging.


In this session, you will explore best practices for setting up and facilitating online meetings, including choosing the right technology, creating structured agendas, and fostering an inclusive atmosphere. You’ll learn how to engage participants through interactive tools like polls, breakout sessions, and visual aids, ensuring everyone has a voice and feels connected.


By the end of this lecture, you will have actionable techniques to enhance the effectiveness of your online meetings, driving higher engagement and better outcomes for your team. Whether you're new to remote management or seeking to refine your skills, this session will empower you to lead online meetings that are not only productive but also enjoyable for all participants.

response.description:
"Which Types of Candidates You Should Never Select During Interviews" is designed to help hiring managers navigate the complex landscape of recruitment by identifying key characteristics that signal a poor fit. Research indicates that up to 30% of new hires fail within the first 18 months due to mismatches in skills, values, or attitude. Moreover, the cost of a bad hire can exceed 150% of the employee's annual salary when considering turnover, lost productivity, and training costs.


In this session, you will learn about specific candidate traits to be wary of, such as a lack of enthusiasm, poor communication skills, and an inability to demonstrate adaptability. You’ll discover how to recognize warning signs during interviews and assess cultural fit beyond the technical qualifications.


By the end of this lecture, you will be equipped with the knowledge to make informed hiring decisions that contribute to a stronger team and a healthier work environment. This session will empower you to enhance your recruitment process, ultimately leading to improved employee retention and overall organizational success. Whether you're an experienced recruiter or new to the hiring process, this lecture will provide valuable insights into creating a high-performing team.

response.description:
"Strategies to Identify Talent as a Hiring Manager During Recruitment" focuses on essential techniques for uncovering high-potential candidates in a competitive hiring landscape. Research indicates that 75% of hiring managers admit to making poor hiring decisions, while companies that implement structured interview processes can improve their quality of hire by 70%.


In this session, you will explore various strategies for identifying talent, including crafting targeted job descriptions, utilizing behavioral interview techniques, and employing skills assessments. You'll learn how to ask the right questions that reveal a candidate's true potential, cultural fit, and ability to contribute to team dynamics.


By the end of this lecture, you will be equipped with actionable insights and tools to refine your recruitment process and enhance your ability to spot the right talent. This session will empower you to build a strong and effective team that aligns with your organization’s goals and values, ensuring long-term success. Whether you're a seasoned hiring manager or new to recruitment, these strategies will elevate your talent identification skills and contribute to your organization's growth.
response.title: Beyond the Resume: Strategies for Identifying Top Talent

response.description:
"Boosting Your Interview Skills as a Hiring Manager" is designed to equip you with the essential techniques to conduct effective interviews that not only assess candidates' qualifications but also align with your organization's culture and values. Studies show that hiring managers who receive interview training improve their hiring decisions by 40%, and structured interviews are twice as effective in predicting candidate success compared to unstructured ones.


In this session, you will delve into key interviewing techniques, such as crafting effective questions, using behavioral interviewing methods, and creating a positive candidate experience. You’ll learn how to read non-verbal cues and assess soft skills that are critical for team dynamics, helping you make more informed hiring decisions.


By the end of this lecture, you will have practical tools and strategies to enhance your interview skills, leading to better hiring outcomes and reduced turnover rates. This session will empower you to approach interviews with confidence, ensuring you select candidates who will thrive in your organization. Whether you are a seasoned hiring manager or new to the recruitment process, this lecture will provide invaluable insights to elevate your interviewing capabilities.
response.title: The Art of Interviewing: Skills to Attract Top Talent

response.description:
"Strategic Questions to Ask During the Interview with Your Candidates" focuses on the art of crafting and asking the right questions to reveal candidates' true capabilities, motivations, and fit for your organization. Research indicates that 70% of hiring decisions are based on the candidate's responses to interview questions, and effective questioning can increase the quality of hire by up to 80%.


In this session, you will learn how to formulate strategic questions that go beyond standard inquiries, such as "Tell me about yourself." You’ll explore techniques for using behavioral and situational questions that allow candidates to showcase their problem-solving abilities, leadership potential, and cultural fit. Additionally, you’ll discover how to tailor your questions to assess not only technical skills but also soft skills essential for collaboration and adaptability.


By the end of this lecture, you will have a toolkit of strategic questions and techniques to conduct more effective interviews that lead to better hiring outcomes. This session will empower you to identify the right talent who aligns with your organizational values and contributes to a thriving workplace culture. Whether you are a new hiring manager or a seasoned recruiter, these insights will enhance your interviewing process and help you build a stronger team.
response.title: Powerful Questions: Unlocking Candidate Potential in Interviews

Leaders with a strong personal brand are 70% more likely to attract new business opportunities and career growth (Forbes, 2023). LinkedIn is your platform to turn expertise into influence. This lecture shows how to craft an engaging leadership story, post impactful content, and connect with the right audience. Learn how top leaders are transforming their networks into thriving communities while positioning themselves as thought leaders in their industry.

Key Skill: Strategic Leadership Branding on LinkedIn

response.description:
"Developing Strategic Planning and Vision as a Leader" is designed to empower you with the skills to create a clear and actionable strategic plan that aligns with your organization's goals. Studies show that organizations with a defined strategic vision experience a 50% higher growth rate, and leaders who engage their teams in the planning process see a 40% increase in employee commitment.


In this session, you will learn the fundamental principles of strategic planning, including setting long-term objectives, analyzing your organization's strengths and weaknesses, and identifying opportunities and threats in the market. You will explore techniques for developing a compelling vision that inspires and motivates your team, ensuring everyone understands their role in achieving shared goals.


Additionally, you’ll discover how to effectively communicate your vision to stakeholders and implement strategies that adapt to changing circumstances, allowing for flexibility and resilience in execution. By the end of this lecture, you will have the tools to create a robust strategic plan that not only charts the course for your organization but also fosters a culture of collaboration and innovation. This session will prepare you to lead with confidence, clarity, and purpose in today’s dynamic business environment, ensuring sustainable success for your team and organization.
response.title: Leading with Purpose: Developing a Strategic Plan and Vision

response.description:
"Developing a Culture That Turns an Average Team into a Great Team" focuses on the critical role of organizational culture in enhancing team performance and cohesion. Research shows that teams with strong cultural alignment experience a 30% increase in productivity, and organizations with engaged teams are 21% more profitable.


In this session, you will learn how to identify and nurture the core values and behaviors that define a high-performing team culture. You will explore practical strategies for fostering collaboration, open communication, and trust among team members, creating an environment where individuals feel valued and motivated to contribute their best.


Additionally, you’ll discover techniques for recognizing and rewarding team achievements, which can significantly boost morale and commitment. You’ll also learn how to address and overcome common cultural barriers that hinder performance, transforming an average team into a powerhouse of innovation and effectiveness.


By the end of this lecture, you will have actionable insights to implement in your organization, empowering you to create a culture that not only enhances performance but also attracts and retains top talent. This session will inspire you to lead with intention and purpose, ensuring that your team thrives and excels in achieving its goals.
response.title: From Average to Outstanding: Cultivating a High-Performance Team Culture
Congratulations! You have completed !
